purpose: The commit adds a guard in hci_cmd_sync_queue that checks if the HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set on the hci_dev structure. If the flag is present, the function immediately returns -ENODEV, preventing further command queuing. This change stops a use‑after‑free scenario that could occur when commands are queued after the device has been unregistered and its memory freed.

The commit removes an unnecessary include of <asm/termios.h> from drivers/tty/n_hdlc.c. The file already includes <linux/tty.h>, which pulls in <linux/termios.h>, making the asm include redundant and potentially confusing. The change simply deletes that line.

This commit updates the perf-c2c documentation to include a new 'N/A' metric for store operations, reflecting changes in the report table. It adds explanatory text and adjusts existing sections to incorporate the new metric, but makes no changes to source code or configuration.

This patch fixes several bugs in the hugetlb subsystem related to VMA lock handling during VMA splitting and range unmapping. It ensures that a new VMA lock is allocated for each VMA when VM_MAYSHARE is set, prevents double freeing of the lock, correctly releases and frees the lock during unmapping, and updates the pmd sharing logic to require the lock. The changes also add missing lock handling for VM_MAYSHARE vmas that were previously omitted, thereby preventing future race conditions.

The commit updates the device tree binding for the Realtek rt5682s codec by adding documentation for two new power supply properties, AVDD-supply and MICVDD-supply, and marking them as required. It also provides example usage of these properties in a device tree snippet.
Adds a missing iounmap() call in the zorro7xx_remove_one() function to correctly unmap memory that was mapped during probe, preventing a resource leak when the device is removed.
The commit removes an unused work_struct field from the mlx5e_async_ctx structure in the kTLS transmit path, cleaning up the data structure.

The commit changes the type of the key_idx field in the cfg80211_auth_request structure from an unsigned 8‑bit integer (u8) to a signed 8‑bit integer (s8). This change is made to avoid static analysis warnings that arise when key_idx can be set to -1 (indicating that the key is NULL). The commit does not add functionality, remove code, or fix a specific bug; it simply improves code quality by making the type appropriate for its intended range.

The commit removes the line '# CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS is not set' from two RISC‑V defconfig files, effectively re‑ordering the configuration options so that the presence or absence of CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS is listed in the same position as produced by savedefconfig. This is a cosmetic change to the ordering of configuration entries and does not alter any functional behavior.

The commit introduces a static inline helper function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() that checks whether the MCP251XFD controller is operating in CAN-FD mode or listen‑only mode. It replaces several inline checks of the ctrlmode bitmask in the FIFO and ring initialization code, simplifying the logic and making it reusable.

The commit modifies the RTC self-test to skip tests when the RTC device is absent. It removes the mandatory assertion in the fixture setup and adds checks before each test that skip the test if the RTC file cannot be opened (errno ENOENT). This prevents test failures on systems without an RTC device.

The commit removes a redundant assignment to the local variable 'bytes' in the function snd_echo_midi_output_write. The variable was set to 0 but never read before being overwritten, so the assignment is dead code. The change deletes the assignment and keeps the rest of the function unchanged, thereby eliminating a clang deadstore warning.

This commit removes the unconditional dependency of the rcuscale test on the RCU Tasks subsystem. It updates the Kconfig to stop selecting TASKS_RCU for the RCU_SCALE_TEST configuration, adds conditional compilation in rcuscale.c so that the tasks_ops structure is only included when CONFIG_TASKS_RCU is enabled, and adjusts the self‑test configuration files to use CONFIG_FORCE_TASKS_RCU instead of CONFIG_TASKS_RCU. The result is that the rcuscale scalability test can now be built and run on kernels that do not have the RCU Tasks subsystem, reducing kernel size and simplifying tracing.
The commit removes a duplicated phrase "be split" from a comment in arch/x86/kvm/mmu/spte.h, correcting a typo in documentation.

The commit reverts a previous change that populated the DCN315 clock table based on dcfclk values. The original change caused a potential underflow when pmfw disabled dcfclk states, leading to a read failure. By reverting, the commit prevents this future bug until a proper solution is implemented.

Adds a compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L (R9A07G054) SoC to the Mali‑Bifrost GPU devicetree bindings, updating the documentation to include the new compatible entry and adjusting the enum list accordingly.

This commit renames the struct previously called `pcie_port` to `dw_pcie_rp` throughout the DW PCIe core driver. All references, function signatures, and type definitions are updated accordingly to maintain consistency with the existing naming convention that prefixes local PCIe structures with `dw_` and suffixes endpoint-related ones with `_ep`. The change is purely a refactoring for code clarity and consistency, with no functional changes or new features added.

The commit changes the format string used in a debug print statement from "%#06hx" to "%#06x" to correctly match the promoted integer type of the argument. This fixes a clang -Wformat warning and ensures the printed protocol value is represented correctly.

The commit corrects a signed/unsigned comparison bug in the MIPS jump_label range check. It casts the upper bound BIT(25) to a signed long so that negative offsets are compared correctly, preventing a false warning.

This commit removes the HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD case from the SetHwReg8188EU() function and eliminates the corresponding enum entry. All calls that previously used SetHwReg8188EU with this variable are replaced with direct calls to rtl8188e_set_p2p_ps_offload_cmd. The change is a refactoring to simplify the HAL layer by eliminating an unnecessary wrapper.

The commit changes the declaration of the array `cavium_erratum_23154_cpus` from a global const to a static const, thereby silencing sparse warnings about the symbol not being declared static. This change limits the symbol’s visibility to the translation unit, preventing accidental external use.

The commit updates the device‑tree binding documentation for Qualcomm MSM display (MDSS) nodes. It removes the bogus interrupt‑flags cell from the "interrupts" property, changing entries like "<0 IRQ_TYPE_LEVEL_HIGH>" or "<0 IRQ_TYPE_NONE>" to just "<0>". This reflects the fact that the MDSS interrupt provider is a single cell and does not support interrupt flags, thereby correcting the documentation to match the actual hardware interface.

This commit refactors the V4L2 control reference handling to allocate space for arrays in the ctrl_ref structure. It renames dynamic‑array specific fields to generic array fields, updates allocation logic, and adjusts all related functions and comments to use the new names. The change prepares the code for future support of driver‑controlled array sizes.

This commit extends the Qualcomm socinfo driver by adding support for additional PMICs and SoCs. It inserts new entries into the pmic_models array and the soc_id table, enabling the driver to recognize and report these devices on supported boards.

The commit rewrites rtl8xxxu_print_chipinfo to correctly map the chip_cut field (0–15) to the corresponding letter A–P. It replaces a switch that only handled 0–4 and printed "unknown" for others with a simple calculation that always produces a valid letter.

The commit removes the custom software enable flag (is_enabled) from the PRCMU clock driver. It deletes the field from the struct, the enable/disable/is_enabled helper functions, and all references to them in the clk_ops tables. The driver already tracks enable counts internally, so the redundant state is eliminated, simplifying the code.

This commit reorganizes the RISC-V mm init code to reduce the number of #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL blocks. It moves the pt_ops macro definition into the same #ifdef block that contains other XIP-related macros and groups the first type of XIP usage cases together. It also moves the extern declarations of _xiprom, _exiprom, and __data_loc into the XIP block. The change is purely a refactor aimed at improving code readability and maintainability by consolidating conditional compilation sections.

This commit modifies the device tree for the sm6350‑sony‑xperia‑lena platform to enable the QUP (Quad Peripheral Interface) and GPI DMA (General Purpose Input/Output Direct Memory Access) hardware blocks. It does so by setting the status property of the gpi_dma0, gpi_dma1, qupv3_id_0, and qupv3_id_1 nodes to "okay", thereby allowing these blocks to be used by the kernel. The change is purely a configuration tweak intended to prepare the platform for future I2C node support.

This commit updates comments in the rzg2l thermal driver: it changes the macro comment to better describe its purpose, corrects a typo in "calibration", and reformats comment blocks to follow coding style guidelines. No functional code changes are made.

The commit replaces an infinite loop that waited for a hardware semaphore in igc_release_swfw_sync_i225 with a single check that logs an error and returns if the semaphore cannot be acquired. This prevents the driver from hanging when the hardware is unexpectedly removed.

This commit updates the pcm3168a ASoC codec driver to replace the legacy master/slave terminology with the modern provider/consumer terminology used in the ASoC DAI format. It changes the struct field name from master_mode to provider_mode, updates local variables, switch statements, error messages, and related logic to use the new constants (SND_SOC_DAIFMT_CLOCK_PROVIDER_MASK, SND_SOC_DAIFMT_CBC_CFC, SND_SOC_DAIFMT_CBP_CFP). The functional behavior of the driver remains the same; only the naming and terminology are modernized for consistency with the rest of the kernel. No new features are added and no bugs are fixed or prevented beyond the cosmetic refactor.

This commit removes a dead macro definition for IS_SWAPFILE that was only defined as a fallback when the macro was not already defined. Since IS_SWAPFILE is always defined in the NFS codebase, the fallback macro is unnecessary and is deleted, simplifying the code.

This commit implements GTP echo request handling by adding code to generate and send echo responses for both GTPv0 and GTPv1 when the GTP device is created in the kernel (i.e., no userspace daemon). It introduces new GTP message types, information element structures, and a restart counter field in the gtp_dev structure. The commit also adds a new netlink attribute for the restart counter, updates routing helper functions, and adjusts the device initialization and attribute handling to support the new fields.

This commit replaces uses of the cpumask_next_* family of functions with the more efficient cpumask_first_* equivalents where appropriate. It adds a new helper cpumask_first_zero() to find the first unset CPU in a mask, and updates several kernel files (blk-mq.c, virtio_net.c, qbman portal drivers, and clocksource.c) to use the new functions. The change is a refactor aimed at improving performance and code clarity, without adding new functionality or fixing a specific bug.

This commit removes the code that queries the firmware for DTIM information via the "dtim_assoc" IOVAR in the brcmfmac driver. The firmware no longer supports this IOVAR, so the call always fails and spams the console after roaming. By eliminating the call, the driver no longer attempts an unsupported operation and the error messages disappear.

This commit adds support for a new Intel platform configuration named "adl_rt5682" that uses the alc5682 headset codec without a speaker amplifier. It introduces a new board ID entry in sof_rt5682.c and updates the ACPI match table in soc-acpi-intel-adl-match.c to include the new configuration, enabling the system to load the appropriate firmware and topology files for this hardware.

The commit adds a guard in io_sendmsg_zc to return -EOPNOTSUPP when the socket does not support zerocopy (SOCK_SUPPORT_ZC flag). This mirrors a previous change that prevented zerocopy send requests on unsupported protocols, ensuring that zerocopy sendmsg behaves correctly for unsupported sockets.

The commit modifies the Sphinx preamble for kernel documentation to add a check for the presence of the xeCJK.sty package before attempting to load it. It introduces a boolean flag, tests for the file, and updates the note shown when CJK fonts or the xeCJK package are missing. The change prevents a build failure on systems where texlive-xecjk is not installed, and updates documentation to reflect this optional dependency.

The commit updates the checkpatch.pl script to suppress error messages for macros that start with "case". It adds a regex to skip lines beginning with "case" so that such macros are not flagged as having complex values. This addresses a false‑positive linting issue in the script.

This commit replaces the legacy simple-KMS helper functions in the simpledrm driver with the newer, more modular atomic helpers. It removes the old simple display pipe structure, introduces a primary plane, CRTC, encoder, and connector using the atomic framework, and updates the mode configuration and plane/CRTC helper functions accordingly. The change is purely architectural and does not alter the driver’s external behavior.

This commit adds a JPEG decode worker interface to the MediaTek JPEG driver for MT8195, enabling three hardware decoders to run concurrently. It introduces new helper functions for hardware allocation, state tracking, and synchronization, updates the core driver to use the new worker, and registers a new device variant for the decoder.

This commit removes a series of VPRINTK() debug logging calls and the ata_scsi_dump_cdb() helper function from the libata subsystem. The changes delete the calls that printed various debug information (e.g., SG mapping, SCSI command types, PRD entries, etc.) and the function that dumped SCSI command descriptors. The intent is to eliminate redundant or unnecessary debug output that is already covered by tracepoints or other logging mechanisms, thereby cleaning up the codebase.

This commit adds support for the "extended blank" optimization in AMD's DRM display driver. It introduces a new flag and data structures to enable zstate (z9/z10) entry during the extra vblank period that is gained when the display enters FREESYNC_STATE_VIDEO. The changes add a new capability flag (zstate_support), a new debug option (extended_blank_optimization), and a new field in the stream update structure to carry timing adjustments. Functions are added to determine if extended blank is supported, to program the extended blank register, and to calculate optimized timing parameters. The logic is integrated into bandwidth optimization, DML calculations, and hardware sequencing for DCN20/31. The commit also updates resource construction to enable the new capability on DCN31. Overall, it implements a new feature that allows the driver to use the extra vblank time to enter zstate when conditions are met.

The commit updates the function pointer prototype for hqd_destroy in kgd_kfd_interface.h, changing the third parameter type from uint32_t to enum kfd_preempt_type. This aligns the prototype with the actual implementations, eliminating a Control Flow Integrity (CFI) violation that occurred when the kernel was compiled with CONFIG_CFI_CLANG on certain GPUs.

This commit updates the device tree binding YAML for ST STM32 pinctrl. It corrects the property name from 'reset' to 'resets' and adds an 'additionalProperties: false' constraint to the patternProperties section, ensuring that only the documented properties are allowed.

The commit adds a conditional compile flag to the ath9k driver Makefile, applying -Wno-array-bounds to CFLAGS_mac.o when KBUILD_EXTRA_WARN is not defined. This suppresses the GCC 12 array-bounds warning that appears in ath9k_hw_resettxqueue, effectively silencing the warning for non‑W=1+ builds.

The commit replaces the use of pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get in the sun8i-ss PRNG driver. It removes an unnecessary pm_runtime_put_noidle call that was executed on error, simplifying the runtime PM handling and ensuring the device is correctly resumed and its usage count is managed.

The commit refactors io_uring fixed file handling by extracting the installation and removal logic into dedicated helper functions that operate on io_ring_ctx instead of io_kiocb. It introduces __io_fixed_fd_install and io_fixed_fd_remove, updates __io_close_fixed to use these helpers, and adjusts related headers and calls in rsrc.c. The changes improve code organization, readability, and maintainability without adding new functionality.

The commit updates the devicetree bindings for the NXP i.MX8MQ VPU to support separate G1 and G2 decoder nodes. It changes the compatible property to allow distinct G1 and G2 identifiers, reduces the reg, interrupts, and clocks arrays to single items per node, removes reg-names, interrupt-names, and clock-names, and adds power-domain references for each decoder. The examples are updated to illustrate the new node structure.

The commit removes the macro IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON, which was only referenced once inside the IWL_DEVICE_BZ macro, and inlines its contents directly into IWL_DEVICE_BZ. This is a straightforward refactoring that cleans up an unused macro definition.
